channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kesha condemns White House for using her song in military TikTok video
item.description: Singer Kesha has publicly criticised the Trump administration for using her 2011 hit ‘Blow’ in a TikTok video posted by the White House that highlighted the “lethality” of the United States armed forc...

item.title: Elijah Blue Allman faces multiple arrests in New Hampshire over burglary and assault incidents
item.description: Elijah Blue Allman, 49, the son of singer Cher and the late musician Gregg Allman, was arrested twice within the same weekend in New Hampshire. On Sunday, authorities responded to a report of a break-...

item.title: Calgary man faces sentencing for severe child abuse of eight-year-old stepson
item.description: In Calgary, a disturbing case of child abuse unfolded in court as Justice Ken McLeod considers a suitable sentence for 41-year-old Matthew Jason Scott. Scott pleaded guilty last spring to aggravated a...

item.title: ECP requests army deployment in Haripur after CM Afridi’s controversial remarks on by-polls
item.description: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) Chief Minister Sohail Afridi recently clarified his remarks regarding the upcoming NA-18 by-election, after the Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) called for the deployment ...

item.title: Three Health Department officials charged over irregular R1m tender to new company
item.description: A company named Ithani Amen, registered only eight days before receiving a R1 million tender from the National Department of Health, is at the centre of a procurement scandal. The firm, which did not ...

item.title: Central Government Officials Hold 1-Hour ‘Seva Sankalp’ Pledge and Discussion
item.description: On Monday, 2nd March 2026, all central government officials across departments set aside an hour to read and discuss the three-page Seva Sankalp Resolution adopted by the Union Cabinet. The pledge, he...

item.title: Australians stranded in the Middle East urged to wait for commercial flights
item.description: Australians currently stranded in the Middle East due to flight cancellations and airspace restrictions have been issued a new travel alert by the Federal Government. Foreign Affairs Assistant Ministe...

item.title: Trump to attend White House Correspondents’ Dinner after years of absence
item.description: Donald Trump has announced that he will attend the White House Correspondents' Dinner on 25 April, ending a long-standing boycott of the event. He made the announcement on his social media platform, c...

item.title: Akinwole II Omoboriowo Becomes Vice Chairman of Botswana Power Corporation
item.description: Botswana don finally appoint Nigerian energy expert, Akinwole II Omoboriowo, as Vice Chairman and Board Member for Botswana Power Corporation (BPC). Na big move wey dey come as Botswana dey try solve ...

item.title: Vincent Massey Trojans stage third-period comeback to win Game 1 of WHSHL final
item.description: The Vincent Massey Trojans overcame a two-goal deficit in the third period to secure a thrilling 3-2 victory over the Oak Park Raiders in the opening game of the Winnipeg High School Hockey League’s D...

item.title: Low Turnout and Tensions in Isiolo and Kakamega By-Elections
item.description: On Thursday, voters in four constituencies across Isiolo, Kakamega and Embu counties went to the polls for by-elections. The Isiolo South parliamentary seat became vacant after the death of MP Mohamed...

item.title: PM Shehbaz defers Moscow visit due to evolving regional security situation and internal challenges
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has postponed his scheduled visit to Russia in light of the prevailing regional and domestic situation, according to an official statement issued by the Prime Minister’s ...

item.title: How to Effectively Present Your Case for a Salary Increase
item.description: Dis article talks about how workers fit in Tanzania and anywhere else can ask for salary raise without stress or drama. First thing, you need to think differently: a raise no be reward for loyalty or ...

item.title: Indian Carriers Resume Relief Flights Amid US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Amid the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, India has initiated measures to ensure the safe return of its citizens stranded in the Middle East. On Tuesday, March 3, 2026, IndiGo o...

item.title: Civilian Helicopter Crashes in Pililla, Rizal, Killing Two and Injuring Three
item.description: A civilian helicopter crashed in Upper Barak, Quisao, Pililla, Rizal on the morning of March 3, 2026, at 7:27 a.m., resulting in two fatalities and three injured passengers. The Rizal Provincial Polic...

item.title: Declassified Granted Parliamentary Press Access After Seven-Month Dispute
item.description: Declassified has secured access to the UK Parliament after a seven-month dispute with Westminster authorities over its application for a press pass. The independent investigative outlet was initially ...

item.title: Beatrice and Eugenie Face Exclusion from Royal Events Amid Family Scandal
item.description: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie have reportedly been barred from attending Royal Ascot and other forthcoming royal events due to the ongoing scandal surrounding their parents, Prince Andrew and Sarah ...

item.title: Child Labour in Zanzibar’s Coastal Fishing Communities Raises Safety and Education Concerns
item.description: A recent report highlights how child labour remains common in Zanzibar’s coastal communities, especially within fishing and other marine-related activities that support the island’s growing blue econo...

item.title: KSE-100 Rebounds Strongly After Historic Single-Day Drop Amid Global Tensions
item.description: Pakistan Stock Exchange witnessed a strong rebound on Tuesday as the KSE-100 index rose by 3,000.76 points, recovering some of the losses from Monday’s unprecedented single-session drop of over 16,000...

item.title: Canada-India Relations Reset Faces Questions on Foreign Interference
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visited India to mend strained diplomatic relations and promote trade, but a scheduled press conference was cancelled due to time constraints following extended mee...
